title: astragalus lagurus subsp. persicus
intro: Astragalus lagurus subsp. persicus, also known as Persian milkvetch, is a plant species belonging to the pea family, Fabaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Persian milkvetch have been found to provide various health benefits, including immune system enhancement, cardiovascular protection, and anti-aging effects. Persian milkvetch can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or capsules, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
